summary: A TypeScript + PostgreSQL application for creating and arranging guitar neck diagrams with an Excalidraw-inspired workflow.
goals: Provide a blank canvas on new project creation.
Allow users to add, move, resize, and scale neck diagrams.
Support configurable neck options: number of strings, scale length or multiscale angle, number of frets, and capo.
Enable drawing notes with label modes: key, interval, or picking direction.
Persist the last opened project so work continues after closing the app.
Offer searchable navigation through a scale/mode/shape library stored in Postgres.
Provide clear, AI-authored project documentation for users and contributors.
Offer a demo page that showcases the product without requiring login.
Provide a landing page with a brief project description and key links.
Ship a reliable import/export flow backed by automated tests.
Polish the theme palettes and set high-contrast as the default theme.
Support import/export interoperability for Guitar Pro, MIDI, and Reaper-oriented workflows.
Allow users to toggle guitar sound playback so note interactions can be auditioned.
Harden worksheet data structures with clear validation and compatibility rules.
non_goals: Full music theory engine beyond basic interval/key calculations.
known_issues: Bug: PNG/PDF export does not prompt for a filename; users cannot name the file before download.
Bug: PNG/PDF export renders black output in some cases; exports must preserve the diagram colors.
Bug: Resizing the browser viewport does not keep diagrams centered; diagrams drift to the right after resize.
Bug: Fret number labels overlap the diagram title; move the title above the diagram rather than below.
Bug: Selecting 27 frets causes dense fret spacing on narrow diagrams; consider minimum width enforcement or adaptive fret label thinning.
Verified: G major renders correctly (not G# as previously reported). Runtime tested 2026-03-04.
Verified: A harmonic minor needs runtime retest; math traces correctly in code.
Verified: Changing strings to 4 correctly removes out-of-range notes via clampNotesToConfig.
Verified: Reducing frets to 5 correctly removes out-of-range notes via clampNotesToConfig.
Verified: Changing key/scale/position in Diagram panel regenerates notes via buildDiagramNotes.
Verified: Mode dropdown is clickable and functional. Runtime tested 2026-03-04.
Fixed: Theory panel Worksheets sub-section header removed; worksheet controls are now inline within Theory panel.
Fixed: Tab bar now truncates long tab names (max-width 150px, ellipsis), shows full name on hover, wider scroll area, and visible thin scrollbar.

canvas: Plus button adds a new neck at a suggested tile position.
Click empty canvas or press Escape to clear selection (no diagram selected).
Drag without modifiers moves the neck.
Drag interactions must remain smooth during continuous state updates (avoid listener churn mid-drag).
Tiling algorithm suggests placement as more diagrams are added.
When the sidebar is collapsed, show a page outline centered in the visible canvas.
Page outline and header stay aligned with each other and the page bounds.
Page outline bounds account for diagram captions so exports match on-screen layout.
notes: Label modes: key, interval, picking direction.
Global key/scale selection to derive interval and note labels.
Imported notes must be normalized to ensure unique ids (generate missing ids on import).
When sound preview is enabled, placing or triggering a note should play a guitar-like note sample for that pitch.
worksheets: Worksheet JSON must continue to support id, title, sourceRef, and items[] where each item maps 1:1 to a renderable diagram.
Worksheet items must support keyId, scaleId, positionId, optional notes[], and optional config overrides for instrument-specific settings.
Worksheet parsing must validate required fields, reject malformed item shapes, and surface user-facing errors for invalid files.
Worksheet compatibility rules must be documented for 6/7/8/9-string diagrams, including tuning normalization and fret-range constraints.
Worksheet render behavior must remain resilient when theory ids are missing from the library (render with available data, do not crash).
diagram_rendering: Fret number labels must never overlap; when spacing is tight, reduce label density or hide labels to preserve readability.

Implementation guidance: compute the minimum adjacent-fret spacing in pixels, estimate a safe label width (e.g. widest possible label + padding), then render every Nth label where N = ceil(labelWidth / minSpacing); always keep key frets like 12 if desired.
header: Do not display the current date in the header.
library: Search bar and dropdown for scales, positions, keys, modes, shapes.
Remove the non-functional library dropdown menu; keep the search bar.
When searching for a shape, scale, or mode, provide autocomplete suggestions from the database.
If no matching library item exists, inform the user the search is invalid.
Create Diagram uses the current key/scale/position selections and adds a new neck when nothing is selected.
Replace Diagram updates only the currently selected neck and preserves its on-canvas position and size.
documentation: An AI agent should generate Markdown docs in /docs that explain setup, architecture, and workflows.
Include quickstart (install, env vars, database, dev server), data model, and API endpoints.
Document canvas interactions (select, move, resize, scale), export/import, and persistence behavior.
Describe demo mode as a sandbox: edits are allowed but reset on refresh.
Capture deployment notes: GitLab Pages hosting and custom domain neckdiagramstudio.com.
Use clear, concise language and avoid inventing features not in the codebase or PRD.
persistence: Cache latest project in localStorage for offline recovery.
Validate cached project payloads on load; discard or normalize when invalid.
demo: Provide a public demo page accessible without login.
Demo page uses a sandbox sample project with preloaded diagrams; edits reset on refresh.
Show a clear call-to-action to open the full studio from the demo page.
landing: Provide a public landing page accessible without login.
Include a brief description of the project.
Include links to the docs, code repository (GitLab), and the creator's website.
Donations link is optional until a wallet URL is available.
Allow users to choose a theme on the landing page; the selection should persist and apply when entering the app.
Navigation should remain legible on mobile without awkward wrapping (use a compact menu or stacked layout).
Hero copy and CTAs should keep balanced vertical spacing on small screens.
Key links grid should scale to wide screens without excessive empty space.
Show a clear active state in the top navigation.
themes: Default theme for first-time users is high-contrast (when no stored theme is set).
Set the default theme to high-contrast.
OLED blackout theme replaces orange accents with a red/white/gray palette.
Jaffa Cake theme uses a brighter, more vivid orange accent.
export: PDF export must render at 300 DPI for print-ready output.
Page outline (letter paper) must be centered in the viewport, remain correct at all zoom levels, and scale appropriately across screen resolutions.
Support importing and exporting Guitar Pro files (at minimum .gp/.gp5/.gpx where parser support allows).
Support importing and exporting MIDI files (.mid/.midi) with track and timing data mapped to worksheet/diagram note events.
Support Reaper workflow interoperability via MIDI plus Reaper project context export/import metadata (e.g. .rpp sidecar mapping or equivalent).
When format limitations prevent lossless round-tripping, show a clear compatibility warning before import/export completes.
audio_playback: Add a global sound toggle in Settings for note audition playback (on/off).
When enabled, user note interactions (click, place, playback) trigger low-latency guitar note playback.
Playback pitch must respect tuning, string index, capo, and fret position.
Provide a basic instrument/voice option starting with clean guitar as the default.
Sound playback state should persist across sessions.
testing: Add an automated test suite that covers import/export flows for diagrams, pages, and JSON.
Import/export tests must cover invalid JSON and regression cases.
Add API integration tests for /api/projects and /api/library (success + error cases).
Lint scripts for apps/api and apps/web must run real linters (no placeholders) and run in CI.

api: Allowlist CORS origins explicitly; never use wildcard origins with credentials.
Define request body limits and validate untrusted inputs at route boundaries.
headers: Set security headers on API and SPA responses: Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and clickjacking protection (frame-ancestors or X-Frame-Options).
Disable Express x-powered-by and use custom 404 and error handlers that do not leak stack traces in production.
abuse_protection: Add rate limiting for state-changing endpoints or enforce limits at the edge.
secrets: Store secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager; do not commit secrets to the repo.
data: Do not expose database ports publicly in production; restrict access to private networks.

boot: Status starts as loading with project = null and isRemote = true.
Canvas defaults to width 1200, height 800, zoom 1.
Default label mode is key with no library query and library type set to all.
first_time_ready: If a remote last project exists, load it, set the title input to the project title, cache it locally, and mark isRemote true.
If no remote project exists, create a blank project titled "Untitled Neck Diagram", persist remotely, cache locally, and mark isRemote true.
If remote access fails, attempt to load the local cache from neck-diagram:last-project; if valid, use it and mark isRemote false.
If remote access fails and no valid local cache exists, create a local-only project with id local-<uuid> and mark isRemote false.
blank_project_data: Tabs array contains a single tab named "Tab 1" with activeTabId pointing to it.
createdAt and updatedAt are initialized to the current timestamp.
diagram_defaults: New diagram defaults: name "Neck", x=60, y=80, width=520, height=160, rotation=0, layoutMode="grid".
Default neck config: strings=8, frets=12, scaleLength=25.5, capo=0, tuning=[F#, B, E, A, D, G, B, E].
Default neck config continued: displayStandardTuning=false, fretNumberStyle="arabic", showFretNumbers=false, highlightRoot=true, snapToGrid=false, showInlays=true.
Default diagram label mode is key and notes start empty.
ui_prefs: Theme defaults to high-contrast for first-time users unless overridden by neck-diagram:theme.
Sidebar defaults to collapsed unless neck-diagram:sidebar-collapsed is set.
Delete warning defaults to true unless neck-diagram:delete-warning is false.
Panels default open: worksheets, theory, diagram, instrument, settings.
Sound preview defaults to off unless neck-diagram:sound-enabled is set.
storage_keys: UI prefs: neck-diagram:sidebar-collapsed, neck-diagram:delete-warning, neck-diagram:theme, neck-diagram:sound-enabled.

coverage: Boot states: loading, ready, and error transitions.
Remote vs local project sourcing and offline recovery.
UI preference persistence across refresh and hard reload.
Canvas interactions: selection, drag, resize, scale, zoom, and outline alignment.
Tab and diagram state integrity across add, delete, rename, and reorder.
bug_test_matrix: Remote success path loads last project, sets title input, caches locally, and status becomes ready.
Remote empty path creates a blank project, saves remotely, caches locally, and status becomes ready.
Remote error path loads local cache when valid; if invalid, creates a local project and status becomes ready.
Local cache with malformed JSON or schema-invalid data falls back to a new local project without throwing.
Local cache with missing tabs or activeTabId is migrated to a valid tab and active selection.
Selected diagram id referencing a missing diagram clears selection gracefully.
Active tab id referencing a missing tab falls back to the first available tab.
Export operations succeed with zero diagrams and with large diagrams.
Import invalid JSON does not mutate state and surfaces a clear error.
Imported diagram notes without ids are assigned unique ids and remain editable/toggleable.
Import page JSON merges tabs/diagrams without duplicating ids or breaking selection.
Exporting a page JSON uses the export name for the file only and does not rename the current project title.
Importing a page JSON into a non-empty project preserves the existing project title.
PNG/PDF export prompts for a filename before download.
PNG/PDF export preserves diagram colors and does not render black output.
Picking label mode cycles a note through Down -> Up -> removed even when the note previously lacked a picking value.
GET /api/library validates the type query and returns 400 for invalid values instead of a 500.
PATCH /api/projects/:id returns 404 for missing ids and 400 for empty patch bodies instead of throwing.
Transpose setting updates neck diagrams; currently diagrams do not respond when transpose changes.
persistence_tests: Theme toggle persists across refresh and resets to default if storage contains an invalid theme id.
Sidebar collapsed state persists across refresh.
Delete warning toggle persists across refresh.
Local project cache updates on save and is restored on cold start.
interaction_tests: Drag and resize state resets on mouseup and on pointer leave without leaving a stuck drag state.
Dragging remains smooth under continuous state updates; drag listeners are not re-registered mid-drag.
Alt-drag resizes, Alt+Shift preserves aspect ratio, and sizes clamp to min/max.
Canvas zoom changes do not desync selection handles or page outline bounds.
Tiling suggestions place new diagrams without overlap and within visible canvas.
Clicking empty canvas or pressing Escape clears the selected diagram and does not auto-select another.
Create Diagram adds a new neck when none is selected; Replace Diagram updates only the selected neck.
Fret number labels never overlap when enabled; labels thin or hide as spacing shrinks.
regression_checks: Library search results remain stable when switching library type or clearing the query.
Diagram note placement uses normalized tuning and consistent label mode.
Project auto-save does not overwrite local edits when offline.

review_findings: Current worksheet structure is flexible and maps cleanly to canvas diagrams.
Current structure lacks explicit format/version metadata for long-term compatibility.
Current structure relies on runtime guards; formal schema validation should be centralized.
Add optional schemaVersion and sourceFormat fields to improve import/export reliability across GP/MIDI/Reaper workflows.

ux_guidelines: Neck diagrams have clear selection state and resize feedback.
Collapsed sidebar view keeps page outline centered with consistent padding.
Demo page should feel fully interactive but clearly marked as demo mode.
Landing page should be concise, clear, and link-focused.
Responsive layout should avoid awkward nav wrapping and preserve visual balance across breakpoints.
In narrow viewports (≤960px) the sidebar overlays the canvas with correct z-index and width so it renders on top.
quality_tech_debt: Reduce drag listener churn by decoupling drag handlers from frequently-updated state; avoid re-registering listeners on every pointer move.
Profile React renders with React DevTools Profiler (drag, resize, import) and address hotspots/unnecessary re-renders.
Normalize imported notes to always include stable ids (generate missing ids and dedupe).
Validate cached localStorage project payloads against schema and fall back safely when invalid.
Unify schema validation/normalization at all ingress points (API routes, localStorage, import).
Replace placeholder lint scripts with real linting and add API integration tests.
Refactor oversized UI modules (e.g., App.tsx) into focused hooks/components to reduce side-effect coupling.
frontend: Normalize tuning input (case + sharps/flats) before storing so note parsing and labels remain consistent.
hosting: Host the site via GitLab Pages.
Custom domain neckdiagramstudio.com is configured and verified (DNS at GoDaddy; HTTPS via Let's Encrypt).
git_ops: Maintain the demo page on a dedicated GitLab branch (separate from main).
Mirror the GitLab repository to GitHub.

conflict_avoidance: Avoid editing the same file concurrently.
If a change spans multiple areas, coordinate by creating a single owning agent.
Prefer additive changes; minimize large refactors without agreement.

ux_ui_design_review.author: Auto
ux_ui_design_review.signature: UX/UI design review incorporated into PRD — Auto
strengths: Theming: six themes with CSS variables and theme swatches in Settings.
Landing: clear value prop, scroll reveal, hero + features + steps + use cases + CTA; phone-frame visuals and gradient headline on-brand.
Studio layout: header → toolbar → workspace (sidebar + canvas) is logical.
ARIA: tabs use role=tablist/tab and aria-selected; sidebar and panels use aria-expanded/aria-label; delete dialog has role=dialog and aria-modal.
Responsive: 960px breakpoint flips workspace and makes sidebar overlay; landing and docs have breakpoints.
consistency_and_polish: Docs page uses className 'cta-button primary' and 'cta-button ghost' but .cta-button is not defined in styles.css; Docs CTAs are unstyled. Add .cta-button, .cta-button.primary, .cta-button.ghost (or reuse .lp-btn/.lp-cta) so Docs matches the rest of the site.
accessibility: Delete modal has role=dialog and aria-modal but no focus trap or return-focus; add focus trap and restore focus to trigger on close.
Modal has an h4 but no aria-labelledby on the dialog; add aria-labelledby (and optional aria-describedby) pointing at the title.
Add a single 'Skip to main content' link at the top of the app targeting main canvas or toolbar.
Confirm when sidebar is collapsed that no focusable node inside it can receive focus (pointer-events and width are already handled).
information_hierarchy_and_empty_states: Handle status === 'error' explicitly: show a message and actions (e.g. Start blank project, Retry).
Empty-state copy: use 'Use Add Neck to add your first diagram' (and optionally one line about tabs/drag).
Optionally add a minimal loading animation (spinner or bar) to the loading card.
navigation_and_wayfinding: Add a 'Docs' or 'Help' link in the app header to /docs and optionally 'Home' to /.
Consider making the header title/sub (e.g. Neck Diagram Studio) a link to / or /docs.
header_and_title: Add placeholder on the title input when empty (e.g. placeholder='Untitled project').
Consider softening the subtitle 'Iconoclast Aud.io // Neck Diagram Studio' to a more user-facing line (e.g. 'by Iconoclast Aud.io') while keeping the link.
toolbar_and_actions: Add aria-label to zoom buttons (e.g. 'Zoom out', 'Reset zoom', 'Zoom in').
Ensure Import/Export menu items are focusable and have role=menuitem (or use native button/a).
If keyboard shortcuts are added later, surface them in tooltips (e.g. 'Add neck (N)').
sidebar_and_panels: In compact sidebar mode, ensure every icon-only control has an aria-label (and optionally title) that names the panel.
Consider a one-line hint on the canvas when no diagram is selected: 'Open the sidebar to set key and scale.'
canvas_and_diagrams: Add an aria-label to the trash zone when it is used (e.g. 'Drop here to delete').
Optionally show the selected diagram name in the toolbar or sidebar when one is selected.
Later: keyboard move (arrow keys) for the selected diagram.
modals_and_destructive_actions: On delete modal open, focus 'Cancel' (or the first non-destructive focusable element), not 'Delete', so Enter does not confirm by accident.
Keep 'Delete' as the last focusable element so tab order ends on Cancel for keyboard users.
responsive_and_layout: Confirm delete modal does not overflow on very narrow viewports (e.g. 320px) and primary/secondary buttons remain tappable.
Consider minimum touch target size (e.g. 44px) for tab buttons and sidebar toggles on touch devices.
small_polish: If the Nerd Font fails to load, ensure fallback text or aria-label is present so actions are not icon-only without a label.
Consider a system monospace fallback earlier in --font-ui for faster first paint if the Nerd Font is slow.

prioritized_actions.item: Define .cta-button and variants so Docs CTAs are styled consistently.
Handle status === 'error' with a dedicated message and Start blank / Retry.
Delete modal: focus trap, focus Cancel on open, restore focus on close, aria-labelledby on dialog.
Add Skip to main content and a Docs/Help link in the app header.
Title input placeholder when empty; zoom buttons aria-label.
Empty-state copy: 'Use Add Neck to add your first diagram.'
Loading: subtle animation; modal Cancel as default focus; optional keyboard move for selected diagram.

frontend_typescript_review.author: Auto
frontend_typescript_review.signature: TypeScript frontend code review incorporated into PRD — Auto
frontend_typescript_review.summary: Review of apps/web and packages/shared from a TypeScript frontend perspective: architecture, types, state, and maintainability.
strengths: Strict TypeScript (strict: true in tsconfig) and shared types in @shared/types used consistently.
Core domain types (NeckDiagram, Note, ProjectData, Worksheet, LibraryItem) are well-defined and exported from a single package.
Pure helpers live in lib/ (projectData, neckMath, tiling, exportUtils) with clear inputs/outputs and test coverage where it exists.
Router is minimal and explicit (pathname → Landing | Docs | App) with a simple Link component and no heavy framework.
NeckDiagram is a focused presentational component with explicit props and callback contracts.
Constants and magic numbers are named (MIN_WIDTH, DRAG_THRESHOLD, PDF_DPI, etc.) and colocated where used.
Theme and storage keys are centralized; theme id is validated with a type guard (isThemeId).
architecture_and_maintainability: App.tsx is ~4000 lines and owns almost all UI state, effects, and handlers; it is the main maintainability risk. PRD quality_tech_debt already calls out refactoring into focused hooks/components.

Recommend extracting: (1) canvas state and drag/resize/scale logic into a useCanvasInteraction hook, (2) sidebar/panel state and layout into a useSidebar hook, (3) library search and theory selection into a useLibrary hook, (4) export/import flows into a useExportImport hook or small module. Keep App as composition of these.
Consider a single useAppState reducer (or useReducer + context) for project, status, and title to simplify boot and save flows and make status transitions explicit.
Router in main.tsx is fine for current scope; if routes grow, consider a small route config array and a single switch/render.
types_and_validation: Shared types omit scaleLength in some flows (stripScaleLength in projectData); ensure all ingress points (API, localStorage, import) use the same normalization so NeckConfig stays consistent.

parseProjectPayload and normalizeProjectData do runtime validation but return ProjectData without branded/schema types; consider a small validation layer (e.g. zod) at API and localStorage boundaries and keep types in sync with schemas.
Worksheet parsing: current inline checks are adequate; for GP/MIDI/Reaper, add explicit schemaVersion/sourceFormat and validate before merging into app state.

API functions (fetchLastProject, createProject, updateProject, fetchLibrary) use generic fetch and throw on !res.ok; consider parsing response body for error messages and typing the error shape for the UI.
state_and_effects: Boot flow never sets status to 'error'; on network failure the app falls back to local/blank and always sets 'ready'. Either remove the 'error' status from the type or add an error path with setStatus('error') and a dedicated error UI (Start blank / Retry) as in ux_ui_design_review.

Many useState calls in App; drag state is correctly split (dragRef for transient pointer state, local state for mode). Preserve this pattern when extracting hooks: refs for transient/event-driven state, useState for UI-reflected state.
useDebouncedEffect is used for save; ensure cleanup and mount guards (mounted) remain when splitting effects into custom hooks to avoid setState on unmounted component.

localStorage for theme, sidebar, delete warning, worksheets: initialization from storage is correct; consider a small useStoredState(key, parse, serialize) to reduce repetition and centralize validation.
performance_and_bundling: React 18 and Vite are appropriate. Lazy loading is not used; if App or Docs/Landing grow, consider React.lazy for route-level code splitting.

No obvious unnecessary re-renders from context; when splitting App, avoid putting frequently changing state (e.g. canvas zoom, drag) in context that wraps the whole tree. Keep context for coarse-grained values (theme, project id, demo mode).
jspdf is imported at top level in App; if PDF export is behind a menu, consider dynamic import so initial bundle is smaller.
testing_and_tooling: Lint script is a placeholder (echo "(lint)"); replace with eslint (and optionally eslint-plugin-react-hooks) as in PRD testing/quality_tech_debt.
Vitest is present; projectData and exportUtils have tests. Add tests for neckMath, tiling, and for boot/error paths once error UI exists.
No E2E tests in apps/web; consider Playwright or similar for critical flows (load project, add diagram, export) if not already covered elsewhere.

suggestions_prioritized.item: Refactor App.tsx: extract useCanvasInteraction, useSidebar, useLibrary, and useExportImport (or equivalent) and keep App as composition.
Implement status === 'error' path: setStatus('error') when remote and local fallback both fail; show message and Start blank / Retry actions.
Replace placeholder lint script with real ESLint (and run in CI).
Introduce schema validation (e.g. zod) at API and localStorage boundaries; derive TypeScript types from schemas where possible.
API layer: return or throw typed errors (e.g. { message: string, status: number }) so UI can show server messages or retry logic.
Consider useStoredState(key, parse, serialize) for theme, sidebar, delete warning, and worksheets to centralize validation and reduce duplication.
Dynamic import for jspdf when user opens PDF export to reduce initial bundle size.
Route-level code splitting with React.lazy for /docs and /app if bundle size becomes an issue.
